description: Reference: Q&A pairs for memphis-311-faq project
note: Instructor uses these to populate the Custom QA knowledge base. Students do NOT need to modify this file.

pairs.question: What is Memphis 311?
pairs.answer: Memphis 311 is the City of Memphis's non-emergency service request system. Citizens can report issues like potholes, trash collection problems, streetlight outages, and other city service needs by calling 311, visiting memphistn.gov/311, or using the Memphis 311 mobile app.

pairs.question: What are the operating hours for Memphis 311?
pairs.answer: Memphis 311 phone lines are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. Online submissions through the website and mobile app can be made at any time. In-person service at City Hall is available Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 4:30 PM.

pairs.question: How do I report a pothole?
pairs.answer: To report a pothole, call 311 or submit a request online at memphistn.gov/311. Provide the exact location (street name and nearest cross street), the approximate size of the pothole, and whether it's in the road or sidewalk. Include a photo if possible.

pairs.question: How long does it take to fix a pothole?
pairs.answer: Pothole repairs are typically completed within 5 to 10 business days after a report is filed. Priority is given to potholes on major roads and those that pose safety hazards. Severe weather may cause delays.

pairs.question: When is my trash pickup day?
pairs.answer: Trash pickup schedules vary by neighborhood. Visit memphistn.gov/solidwaste or call 311 with your address to find your specific pickup day. Residential trash is collected once per week. Place bins at the curb by 6:00 AM on your collection day.

pairs.question: What items can I recycle?
pairs.answer: Memphis curbside recycling accepts paper, cardboard, plastic bottles and containers (types 1-7), aluminum and steel cans, and glass bottles. Do NOT include plastic bags, Styrofoam, food waste, or electronics. Rinse containers before recycling.

pairs.question: How do I report a streetlight outage?
pairs.answer: Report streetlight outages by calling 311 or online at memphistn.gov/311. You'll need the pole number (usually on a metal tag on the pole), the street address or nearest intersection, and a description of the issue (completely out, flickering, damaged).

pairs.question: How do I check the status of my service request?
pairs.answer: Check your request status online at memphistn.gov/311 using your case number, or call 311 and provide your case number to a representative. You can also track status through the Memphis 311 mobile app if you submitted your request there.

pairs.question: Can I report issues anonymously?
pairs.answer: Yes, you can report issues anonymously through the 311 system. However, providing contact information allows us to follow up with you for additional details and notify you when the issue is resolved.

pairs.question: What do I do about illegal dumping?
pairs.answer: Report illegal dumping by calling 311 or submitting online. Provide the exact location, description of materials dumped, and photos if available. Do not attempt to clean up hazardous materials yourself. The Environmental Court handles enforcement.

pairs.question: How do I report a water leak or broken water main?
pairs.answer: For water emergencies, call Memphis Light, Gas and Water (MLGW) at 901-528-4465 immediately. For non-emergency water issues, you can also report through 311. Always report active leaks as soon as possible to prevent property damage.

pairs.question: What is the city doing about stray animals?
pairs.answer: Memphis Animal Services handles stray animal reports. Call 311 to report a stray animal, or contact Memphis Animal Services directly at 901-636-1416. For aggressive or injured animals, call immediately. Non-emergency strays are picked up based on priority and availability.

pairs.question: How do I request a large item pickup?
pairs.answer: Request bulky item pickup by calling 311 at least 24 hours before your regular trash collection day. Up to 2 large items per week are collected at no extra charge. Items must be placed at the curb. Appliances containing refrigerant require special handling.

pairs.question: Who do I contact about a building code violation?
pairs.answer: Report building code violations through 311 or online. The Code Enforcement division will investigate. Common violations include overgrown lots, abandoned vehicles, and unsafe structures. Provide the address and description of the violation.

pairs.question: How do I get a new trash or recycling bin?
pairs.answer: Request a new or replacement bin by calling 311. There is no charge for standard replacement bins. Delivery typically takes 5-7 business days. If your bin was damaged by the collection crew, note this when making your request for priority service.

pairs.question: What languages does Memphis 311 support?
pairs.answer: Memphis 311 provides service in English and Spanish. Translation services for other languages are available upon request through our phone line. The website and app are currently available in English with plans for multilingual expansion.

pairs.question: How do I report a traffic signal problem?
pairs.answer: Report malfunctioning traffic signals by calling 311. Provide the intersection location and describe the problem (light stuck on red, all lights flashing, completely dark). For immediate safety hazards, also call Memphis Police non-emergency at 901-545-2677.

pairs.question: Can I volunteer for neighborhood cleanup?
pairs.answer: Yes! Memphis organizes regular community cleanup events. Call 311 or visit memphistn.gov/beautification to learn about upcoming events. The city can provide bags, gloves, and arrange for pickup of collected debris for organized cleanup groups.

pairs.question: What is the Memphis 311 mobile app?
pairs.answer: The Memphis 311 app is available for iOS and Android. It allows you to submit service requests with photos, track request status, and receive notifications when your issue is resolved. Download it from the App Store or Google Play by searching 'Memphis 311'.

pairs.question: How do I report graffiti?
pairs.answer: Report graffiti through 311 by phone or online. Provide the location, surface type (wall, fence, sign), and approximate size. The city's graffiti removal team typically responds within 5-10 business days. Graffiti on private property may require owner cooperation.
